title: Loop A: DA objection severity vocabulary mismatch (fatal/major/minor vs counted 'high')
source: 2026-07-04 tick 3: DA returned 2 'fatal' objections; loop_a_tick counted da_objections_high=0
why: scripts/loop_a_tick.py run_preflight counts objections with severity=='high', but the DA agent emits fatal/major/minor. Tick 3's two FATAL objections were recorded as da_objections_high=0 — the daily report understates review severity and any future logic keyed on objection counts would silently treat fatal blocks as benign. Fix: normalize the severity vocabulary in the DA dispatch prompt AND accept both in the counter; add a lint.
scope: Pick one canonical severity enum and enforce it in the DA output contract
loop_a_tick: count {'high','fatal'} (or normalized equivalent) as high-severity
Backfill-safe: re-derive severity counts when rendering, don't persist the buggy count

history.reason: severity normalization + objection-text rendering shipped with tests (tests/test_loop_a_severity.py)
